workouts.name: TCS World 10K Bangalore — Race
workouts.notes: Race day. Splits 5:48 / 6:13 / 6:28 / 6:48 / 9:52 / 6:52 / 8:08 / 7:56 / 6:55 / 7:13. Walked km 5 (bonk after 4km above threshold). Avg HR 178.

raceResults.notes: Goal sub-65 missed (real fitness ceiling ~70 per Riegel from 35:40 5K). Beat Riegel projection by 2:11 despite km-5 bonk. Lessons: km 1 went 5:48 (42s/km too fast), HR pinned at 180+ from km 3, walked km 5. Next race: cap HR 170 first 5k, gel at km 5, even/negative split.

wentWrong: Started at 5:48/km — sub-58 pace on a -8m downhill. Adrenaline + crowd surge.
First 4 km averaged 6:19/km — faster than current 5K PB pace. Aerobic system was over the line by km 3.
HR pinned at 180-181 (93-94% max) for km 3-4. That's 5K effort sustained for 10K = guaranteed late-race collapse.
Km 5 walk break (~3:20 lost vs target pace) — glycogen depletion + leg cramp; HR dropped 5 bpm confirming a stop.
Second half never recovered: km 7-8 at 8:00+ pace with HR still elevated = lactate hangover.
wentRight: Beat Riegel 5K-to-10K projection by 2:11 (predicted 74:24, ran 72:13). Engine is fitter than the 5K time alone suggests.
Km 9-10 effort climbed despite the second-half collapse — residual fight in the tank.
No injury, no DNF, completed the distance in race conditions.
fixForNextRace: Hard HR cap: 170 bpm for first 5km. Currently went above 175 by km 2.
First-km governor: target +10s slower than goal pace, not faster. Train this in tempo workouts.
Fuel: 30g carb 60min pre + one gel at km 5. The bonk lined up with glycogen depletion window.
Negative-split protocol: first 5k at goal pace + 5s/km, second 5k at goal pace - 5s/km.
Weight: every kg off is ~3 sec/km. 93 to 85 alone is worth ~4 min on the 10K.
postMortem.realisticNextTarget: 70:00 by Bangalore Marathon prep block (Sept 2026) at current weight. Sub-65 requires 5K PB to drop to ~31:00 (currently 35:40) AND/OR weight at 82-85 kg.
